title: Roasted Smoky Ukrainian Grain Pilaf
description: A malty, crispy, and smoky plant-based Ukrainian dish featuring tahini paste, this pilaf is perfect for a monsoon evening, it combines the flavors of roasted grains and vegetables with a hint of smoke and heat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together tahini paste, smoked paprika, hot sauce, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and mushrooms to the bowl and toss to coat with the tahini mixture.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the buckwheat groats to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until lightly toasted.
Add the vegetable mixture to the skillet and stir to combine with the buckwheat.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the skillet from the oven and stir in the minced garlic.
Return the skillet to the oven and roast for an additional 5 minutes, or until the pilaf is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
